pages.title: Clive Lewis, Labour MP for Norwich South - Clive Lewis
pages.h1: Clive Lewis, Labour MP for Norwich South
headings: h3: Contact details
h3: Further details
pages.text_excerpt: Clive Lewis, Labour MP for Norwich South - Clive Lewis Skip to main content Clive Lewis For Norwich South Join Get Involved Donate Latest News About Clive Commentary and Analysis Can you help me and Labour in Norwich? Campaigns Standing up for the victims of the Infected Blood Scandal Backing a more inclusive education system for SEND children Campaigning for decent, accessible dental services in Norwich TOGETHER WE SAVED OUR WALK-IN CENTRE! Campaigning for decent mental health care in Norwich and Norfolk Standing up for our city’s military veterans Stop Redundancies at the University of East Anglia Campaigning to protect and improve public services in Norwich Say NO to parking unfairness at Earlham house Save our Services Keep our Schools and Children Safe from Fire Save our Post Offices Scambassador Save Our Community Pharmacies Save our Pharmacy! Lloyds Pharmacy – Colman Road Join Labour’s campaign to SAVE OUR NHS Contact Clive Privacy Policy More Search Search the website Search Menu Clive Lewis, Labour MP for Norwich South Clive Lewis MP speaking at a local event Listening to you, it’s clear we share the same desire for a fully funded public, not private, NHS; for decent, secure, properly paid jobs; an economy that works for all of us, not just a few and where the wealthy pay their way. Here in Norwich, I’ve campaigned against the privatisation of local NHS services, been at the centre of a broad coalition of trade unions, faith groups, businesses and academics working for a Living Wage across the city, and worked with local campaign groups and trade unions for public ownership of the failing privatised rail industry. I’m passionate about Norwich’s development of scientific centres of excellence. I support institutions such as the Norwich Research Park, which aims to develop the city’s future by creating and supporting new companies and jobs based on world-leading bioscience and renewable technology. But despite all that, I’m not your typical politician. My life outside of politics has given me experience of the real world. For more than a decade, I was a BBC TV news reporter, eventually becoming the BBC eastern region’s chief political reporter. After that, I worked as a BBC technician. I’ve also been an army reservist infantry officer, serving a tour of duty in Afghanistan in 2009. I’m also a governor of Lakenham Primary School and Nursery in my constituency and have worked with other schools and charities to help some of our city’s most disadvantaged children. I was also one of the last Labour Government’s National Black Role Models, working with young people across the UK. Born in London, I grew up on a council estate in Northampton. I attended a local comprehensive, becoming the first family member to attend university. I read economics at the University of Bradford before being elected student union president and Vice President of the National Union of Students (NUS). Ultimately, I’ll always speak my mind and try to do what is best for Norwich and you can read more about my local and regional priorities here . Clive Lewis Contact details Norwich, UK View on Google Maps Email: casework@clivelewis.org Further details Promoted by David Fullman on behalf of Clive Lewis, all at St Marks Church Hall, Hall Road, Norwich, NR1 3HL. ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: Campaigns - Clive Lewis
headings: h3: Contact details
h3: Further details
pages.text_excerpt: Campaigns - Clive Lewis Skip to main content Clive Lewis For Norwich South Join Get Involved Donate Latest News About Clive Commentary and Analysis Can you help me and Labour in Norwich? Campaigns Standing up for the victims of the Infected Blood Scandal Backing a more inclusive education system for SEND children Campaigning for decent, accessible dental services in Norwich TOGETHER WE SAVED OUR WALK-IN CENTRE! Campaigning for decent mental health care in Norwich and Norfolk Standing up for our city’s military veterans Stop Redundancies at the University of East Anglia Campaigning to protect and improve public services in Norwich Say NO to parking unfairness at Earlham house Save our Services Keep our Schools and Children Safe from Fire Save our Post Offices Scambassador Save Our Community Pharmacies Save our Pharmacy! Lloyds Pharmacy – Colman Road Join Labour’s campaign to SAVE OUR NHS Contact Clive Privacy Policy More Search Search the website Search Menu Campaigns Standing up for the victims of the Infected Blood Scandal The infected blood scandal led to more than 30,000 NHS patients receiving contaminated blood, resulting in the deaths of at least 3000 people. I’m campaigning to make sure that those affected achieve justice. Backing a more inclusive education system for SEND children SEND children across Norwich and Norfolk are not getting the support they need. For these children to succeed, we need to build an inclusive education system where all children can thrive. I’ve been working with campaigners inside and outside Parliament to ensure every child has the right to succeed. Campaigning for decent, accessible dental services in Norwich Dental services in Norwich and Norfolk are in crisis; hundreds of adults and children cannot get the care that they need. I’m campaigning to save dental services in Norwich and Norfolk. TOGETHER WE SAVED OUR WALK-IN CENTRE! Concerned constituents told me about the potential closure of the Rouen Road walk-in centre. So, myself, the Labour Parliamentary Candidate for Norwich North, Alice Macdonald, and Labour activists in Norwich came together to start a campaign to save the centre. Campaigning for decent mental health care in Norwich and Norfolk Norfolk and Suffolk Foundation Trust (NSFT) provides local mental health services. The Care Quality Commission has rated NSFT “inadequate” four times since 2017. Although the rating has recently improved, I still want a public inquiry to investigate how the trust was allowed to fail repeatedly, learning lessons to prevent it from happening again Standing up for our city’s military veterans I’m a former soldier, have served on combat duty in the British Army and take a close interest in issues to do with the welfare of people who serve and who have served in our armed forces. Stop Redundancies at the University of East Anglia The University of East Anglia (UEA) is not only a vital ‘anchor institution’ in Norwich, it’s one of the most important in the Eastern region. In mid-2023, the university announced said it needed to cut over £45 million from its annual budget, meaning over 100 job losses. I have worked and will continue to work with the University College Union and other unions to try and prevent job losses and cuts at UEA. Campaigning to protect and improve public services in Norwich Our public services are vital, and the Conservatives have run them into the ground. Since 2010, combined funding for local services in Norwich and Norfolk has been slashed by well over £1,000,000,000. I have been campaigning to protect and improve public services in Norwich because the fight for high-quality services is part of the collective fight for a better future. Say NO to parking unfairness at Earlham House Signs have appeared at Earlham House shopping centre saying there would be a charge to use the car park from “around the 1st June 2017”. These charges threatened the livelihood and existence of local shops and placed more parking pressure on nearby str
